engineIntelligence.h2: Mazda 626 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Mazda 626 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Mazda 626 FS-DE engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - belt stretch or tensioner wear causes interference damage if not replaced at 60k miles.
Head gasket weakness - coolant mixing with oil on pre-2000 units with high mileage.

cards.altText: Mazda 626 RF engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger failure - worn turbo seals cause oil consumption and loss of power on high-mileage fleet vehicles.
Injector wear - rough running and hard starting from degraded diesel injectors after 100k miles.

cards.altText: Mazda 626 FE engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - well-documented weakness causing overheating and coolant/oil mixing on pre-1997 units.
Timing belt wear - missed service intervals lead to belt failure and valve damage on interference engines.

engineGuide.h2: Mazda 626 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mazda 626 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The RF turbo diesel powered most UK 626 fleet vehicles, offering strong torque but requiring careful maintenance to avoid turbo failure.
compatibleVariants: LX 2.0 TD, GLX 2.0 TD, Estate 2.0 TD, Sedan 2.0 TD, TD Sport
commonFailures: Turbocharger seal wear - oil consumption and power loss typically after 100k miles on high-mileage examples.
Injector degradation - rough idle and hard starting from worn diesel injectors.
EGR valve clogging - reduced performance and increased emissions on short-journey vehicles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for RF Mazda 626 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The earlier WL diesel served pre-1997 GE chassis models, a simpler design but prone to injector issues without regular servicing.
compatibleVariants: LX 2.0 D, GLX 2.0 D, Sedan 2.0 D, Coupe 2.0 D (GE chassis)
commonFailures: Injector pump wear - fuel delivery issues causing poor starting and uneven running on higher-mileage units.
Head gasket weakness - coolant mixing with oil on neglected examples with missed service intervals.
entries.cta: Get quotes for WL Mazda 626 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The most common 626 petrol engine, the FS-DE offered reliable performance but requires timely timing belt changes to avoid catastrophic failure.
compatibleVariants: LX 2.0, GLX 2.0, GLX-E 2.0, Sport 2.0, Estate 2.0, Coupe 2.0, Sedan 2.0
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine design means belt snap causes valve damage; replace every 60k miles.
Head gasket weakness - coolant/oil mixing reported on pre-2000 units with high mileage or overheating history.
Water pump leaks - common wear item that should be replaced alongside timing belt service.
entries.cta: Get quotes for FS-DE Mazda 626 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The FE powered earlier GE chassis 626 models; robust but known for head gasket issues if cooling system maintenance was neglected.
compatibleVariants: LX 2.0, GLX 2.0, GLX-E 2.0, Executive, Coupe 2.0, Sedan 2.0 (GE chassis)
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - well-documented weakness causing overheating; rebuilt units include upgraded gaskets.
Timing belt wear - missed service intervals lead to belt failure on this interference engine design.
entries.cta: Get quotes for FE Mazda 626 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L High-Output Petrol
entries.history: The performance variant of the FS family, fitted to Sport and Coupe models; shares failure patterns with FS-DE but with higher stress components.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear - higher-revving nature accelerates tensioner fatigue; inspect at 50k mile intervals.
Valve guide wear - increased oil consumption on high-mileage performance examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for FS-ZE Mazda 626 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L V6 Petrol
entries.history: The flagship V6 for the 626 range, offering smooth power but requiring specialist sourcing for replacement due to lower production volumes.
commonFailures: Variable intake system faults - swirl valve actuator failures causing reduced low-end torque.
Camshaft position sensor issues - intermittent misfires and rough running on higher-mileage V6 units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for KL-DE Mazda 626 engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Mazda 626 engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Mazda 626 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mazda 626's most common replacement enquiries centre on the FS-DE petrol and RF diesel engines. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your 626.

problems.h4: Mazda 626 Timing Belt Failure - Why FS-DE & FE Petrol Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Mazda 626 LX, GLX, Sport, Estate, Coupe (1992-2002, FS-DE & FE petrol engines)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if not replaced on schedule
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner wear or missed service intervals cause belt stretch; on these interference engines, belt failure bends valves and damages pistons.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers and water pump before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: 60k mile service interval; only viable if engine still runs smoothly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, reseal gaskets; requires machining and testing
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable only if block/pistons undamaged; risk of hidden damage remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FS-DE/FE unit with upgraded belt kit, tested compression, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage 626; eliminates hidden damage risk

problems.recommendation: If your 626 still runs but is due a belt change, the preventative option offers excellent value. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine from our network typically costs less than a dealer head repair and comes with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FS-DE engine prices for your Mazda 626

problems.h4: Mazda 626 Head Gasket Failure - Coolant & Oil Mixing on FE/FS Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mazda 626 GLX, Executive, LX (1987-2000, FE & early FS-DE petrol engines)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles, accelerated by overheating or missed coolant changes
problems.rootCause: Factory head gasket design on pre-2000 units is prone to degradation; overheating or old coolant accelerates failure, allowing coolant into oil passages.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, fit upgraded multi-layer steel gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if block/head are undamaged; risk of warped surfaces on overheated engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FE/FS unit with upgraded gaskets, pressure-tested block, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value for 626 worth £500-£2,000; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: On a 626 valued under £2,000, a rebuilt engine with upgraded gaskets often costs less than a dealer head repair and provides greater peace of mind. Always pressure-test the block before committing to a gasket-only fix.
problems.cta: Get quotes for FE rebuilt Mazda 626 engines with upgraded gaskets

problems.h4: Mazda 626 Diesel Turbo Failure - RF & WL Turbocharger Issues
problems.affectedModels: Mazda 626 LX TD, GLX TD, Estate TD, TD Sport (1997-2002, RF & WL turbo diesel engines)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger seals wear from oil starvation or coked oil passages; loss of boost, blue smoke and oil consumption follow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, refurbish or replace cartridge, clean oil feed lines, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine compression is good and no internal damage occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel unit with tested/refurbished turbo, renewed injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best choice if engine has high mileage or shows signs of internal wear

problems.recommendation: If your 626 diesel has strong compression and no knocking, a turbo refurb may suffice. For fleet vehicles over 120k miles, a rebuilt engine with tested turbo and injectors offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt RF diesel engine prices for your Mazda 626

problems.h4: Mazda 626 Diesel Injector Failure - Rough Running & Hard Starting
problems.affectedModels: Mazda 626 TD variants (1992-2002, RF, RF-Turbo, WL, WL-Turbo diesel engines)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 90,000-130,000 miles, accelerated by poor fuel quality or infrequent servicing
problems.rootCause: Injector nozzles wear or stick from carbon buildup; causes misfires, excessive smoke, poor fuel economy and difficulty starting when cold.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, ultrasonic clean or replace nozzles, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine compression is good and no internal damage from fuel wash

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt RF/WL unit with renewed injectors, tested turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage 626 diesel with multiple wear items

problems.recommendation: Start with a compression test and injector leak-off test. If compression is strong, injector service may resolve the issue. For engines with multiple faults, a rebuilt unit with renewed injectors offers better value.
problems.cta: Find Mazda 626 diesel engines with renewed injectors included

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mazda 626
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above represent the most common reasons UK Mazda 626 owners seek engine replacement. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand these failure patterns. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original weakness, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your 626 back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mazda 626 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mazda 626 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mazda 626 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mazda 626 in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mazda 626 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L diesel was the backbone of the 626 diesel range across GF and GW chassis generations, offered in naturally aspirated WL and turbocharged RF formats for fleet and private buyers seeking strong torque and fuel economy.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo seal wear on RF units; injector degradation on high-mileage WL examples; EGR clogging on short-journey vehicles
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda 626 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda 626 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.8L petrol option powered base LX and GLX trims on GE chassis models, offering adequate performance for budget-conscious buyers but less common in the UK replacement market than the 2.0L units.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt wear on neglected units; valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption on higher-mileage examples

items.title: Mazda 626 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common 626 petrol engine, the 2.0L FS-DE and FE units powered the majority of UK sales across LX, GLX, Sport and Estate variants, balancing performance and running costs but requiring strict timing belt maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure on missed-service units; head gasket weakness on pre-2000 FE engines; water pump leaks alongside belt changes

items.title: Mazda 626 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 2.5L V6 KL-DE offered smooth, refined power for Executive and Sport V6 trim levels, but lower production volumes mean replacement units require specialist sourcing and verification of chassis compatibility.
commonFailurePoints: Variable intake system actuator faults; camshaft position sensor issues on higher-mileage examples

items.descriptor: The FS-DE and FE 2.0L petrol engines powered the majority of UK 626 sales, offering reliable performance but requiring strict timing belt maintenance to avoid catastrophic interference damage.
families: FS-DE - dominant replacement engine (LX/GLX/Sport), timing belt critical at 60k mile intervals

engineYears.h2: Mazda 626 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mazda 626's UK production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from specialists.

years.description: The third-generation GE chassis launched with FE petrol and early WL diesel engines, establishing the 626's reputation for practical family transport.
keyChanges: Introduction of GE chassis platform replacing previous GD generation
FE 2.0L petrol became the mainstream engine across LX and GLX trims
WL 2.0L diesel introduced for fleet buyers seeking improved economy
Coupe body style added to range alongside sedan and estate
knownFor: FE engine robust but head gasket weakness emerges on neglected examples
Simple, easy-to-maintain mechanicals appeal to budget-conscious owners
Early WL diesel reliable but injector pump wear on high-mileage units
GE chassis rust protection less effective than later GF/GW generations
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1991 Mazda 626 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda 626 FE head gasket fix • Mazda 626 WL injector replacement • Mazda 626 1990 engine supply

years.description: Mid-life updates refined the GE chassis, with FS-DE development underway for the next generation while FE and WL remained the workhorse engines.
keyChanges: Facelift updates to front/rear styling and interior trim levels
FP-DE introduced as updated 1.8L petrol option for select markets
WL-Turbo variant offered on higher-spec TD models in limited numbers
Final production years before GF chassis launch in 1997
mainEngines: FE-DOHC - 2.0L high-output petrol (Sport variants, limited UK availability)
popularModels: Mazda Executive V6 (late GE, KL-DE limited availability)
knownFor: FE head gasket failures accelerate on units with missed coolant changes
WL-Turbo offers stronger performance but turbo seal wear common after 100k miles
Coupe body style popular with enthusiasts; engine compatibility same as sedan
Rust protection improvements over early GE but still a consideration for buyers

years.description: The all-new GF (saloon/estate) and GW (sedan) chassis introduced the FS-DE petrol and RF turbo diesel engines that would dominate UK 626 replacement enquiries for decades.
keyChanges: Launch of GF chassis (hatchback/estate) and GW chassis (sedan) platforms
FS-DE 2.0L petrol replaced FE as mainstream engine across LX, GLX, Sport trims
RF 2.0L turbo diesel replaced WL as the primary diesel option for fleet buyers
KL-DE 2.5L V6 offered on Executive and Sport V6 as the performance flagship
mainEngines: FS-DE - 2.0L petrol (LX, GLX, Sport, Estate, Coupe, Sedan)
FS-ZE - 2.0L high-output petrol (Sport, Coupe Sport variants)
RF/RF-Turbo - 2.0L turbo diesel (LX TD, GLX TD, Estate TD, TD Sport)
KL-DE - 2.5L V6 petrol (Executive V6, Sport V6)
knownFor: FS-DE timing belt critical at 60k miles; failure causes expensive valve damage
RF turbo seal wear common on high-mileage fleet vehicles after 100k miles
GF/GW chassis rust protection significantly improved over GE generation
Sport V6 KL-DE offers refined power but specialist sourcing required for replacements

faq.h2: Mazda 626 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mazda 626 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mazda 626 engine typically costs £750-£1,800 supply-only, depending on engine code and condition. Used units start around £400, while reconditioned or specialist V6 rebuilds may reach £2,200.
keyPoints: FS-DE petrol rebuilt: £850-£1,600 typical UK range
RF diesel rebuilt: £950-£1,800 including tested turbo
KL-DE V6 specialist rebuild: £1,450-£2,200 due to lower availability
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£850 depending on garage location
items.cta: Compare Mazda 626 engine prices from UK specialists now

items.question: What engine code is in my Mazda 626 and where do I find it?
items.answer: Your Mazda 626 engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox or on the under-bonnet VIN plate. Common codes include FS-DE (petrol), RF (diesel turbo), FE (early petrol) and KL-DE (V6).
keyPoints: FS-DE: 2.0L petrol, GF/GW chassis 1997-2002, most common replacement
RF: 2.0L turbo diesel, GF/GW chassis 1997-2002, fleet vehicle favourite
FE: 2.0L petrol, GE chassis 1987-1997, head gasket weakness on early units
Check near gearbox housing or VIN plate for stamped code before ordering
items.cta: Verify your Mazda 626 engine code instantly with our registration lookup

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Mazda 626?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 50% of your 626's value (£500-£2,000 typical), a rebuilt engine often provides better long-term value than scrapping, especially with a 12-month warranty included.
keyPoints: Rebuilt FS-DE/RF engines cost less than dealer repairs on high-mileage units
Warranty protection reduces risk versus buying another used 626
Supply & fit options available nationwide for non-technical owners
Consider total cost of ownership versus vehicle's remaining lifespan
items.cta: Get a personalised Mazda 626 repair vs replace quote today

items.question: What are the most common Mazda 626 engine problems?
items.answer: FS-DE petrol engines suffer timing belt failure if not changed at 60k miles; FE units have head gasket weakness; RF diesel turbos wear on high-mileage fleet vehicles; injector issues affect all diesel variants.
keyPoints: Timing belt failure on FS-DE/FE causes expensive valve damage on interference engines
Head gasket degradation on pre-2000 FE engines leads to coolant/oil mixing
RF turbo seal wear causes oil consumption and power loss after 100k miles
Diesel injector degradation causes rough running and hard starting on neglected units
items.cta: Find Mazda 626 engines with failure-point upgrades included

items.question: How long does a Mazda 626 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Standard Mazda 626 engine replacement takes 1-3 days including diagnostics, fitting and testing at approved UK garages. Engine supply alone can be next-day for common FS-DE/RF codes from UK breakers.
keyPoints: Next-day collection available for most GF/GW chassis engines over £600
Supply & fit appointments typically scheduled within 3-5 working days
Complex V6 KL-DE or rare codes may require 5-10 day lead time for sourcing
Post-installation testing and ECU adaptation included in professional fitting
items.cta: Book your Mazda 626 engine fitting appointment with local specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with Mazda 626 replacement engines?
items.answer: All used Mazda 626 engines from our UK breaker network include a minimum 3-month warranty. Reconditioned and rebuilt units typically offer 6-12 months coverage, with unlimited mileage on most rebuilt options.
keyPoints: Minimum 3-month warranty standard on all used engine purchases
Rebuilt units from specialist partners often include 12-month unlimited mileage coverage
Warranty terms vary by supplier; always confirm exact coverage before ordering
Supply & fit packages may include labour warranty alongside engine coverage
items.cta: Compare Mazda 626 engine warranty options from UK suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Mazda 626?
items.answer: Engine swaps on the Mazda 626 require ECU reprogramming, mounting verification and emissions compliance checks. We only supply OEM-spec replacement engines matching your original engine code for guaranteed compatibility.
keyPoints: FS-DE and FE share mounting points but differ in ECU mapping and sensors
RF diesel to petrol conversions require extensive wiring and ECU changes
V6 KL-DE swaps need chassis verification and upgraded cooling components
Stick with original-spec replacements to avoid MOT and insurance complications
items.cta: Get quotes for OEM-spec Mazda 626 engine replacements only

items.question: Why did my Mazda 626 engine fail?
items.answer: FS-DE timing belt failures often stem from missed servicing; FE head gasket issues accelerate with overheating; RF turbo wear results from oil starvation on high-mileage fleet vehicles with infrequent oil changes.
keyPoints: Timing belt replacement every 60k miles critical on FS-DE/FE interference engines
Coolant changes and thermostat maintenance prevent FE head gasket degradation
Regular oil changes and turbo cooldown periods extend RF turbocharger life
Diesel injector service intervals reduce rough running and hard starting issues
items.cta: Find rebuilt Mazda 626 engines with upgraded failure-point components

items.question: How do I know if my Mazda 626 needs a new engine?
items.answer: Persistent knocking, low compression readings, excessive oil consumption or coolant mixing with oil typically indicate the need for replacement. Get a compression test before committing to a full engine swap.
keyPoints: Metallic knocking from bottom end suggests bearing wear requiring rebuild
Compression below 120psi on multiple cylinders indicates worn rings or valves
Coolant in oil or white exhaust smoke points to head gasket failure
Blue smoke and oil consumption signal turbo seal or piston ring wear
items.cta: Get a compression test recommendation from UK Mazda 626 specialists

items.question: Are used Mazda 626 engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Mazda 626 engines from reputable UK breakers carry a 3-month minimum warranty and can be reliable if mileage is verified and compression tested before purchase. Rebuilt units offer greater peace of mind for high-mileage vehicles.
keyPoints: Request compression test results and service history with any used engine
Verify mileage via breaker documentation and DVLA records where possible
Rebuilt units include new wear items and address known failure patterns
Budget-conscious buyers often choose used; long-term owners prefer rebuilt
items.cta: Check used Mazda 626 engine availability with verified mileage and warranty
